hero.mission_description: Establishing a definitive source of truth for architectural contracts, eliminating entropy, and enforcing strict project orchestration.
hero.mcp_definition: Model Context Protocol (MCP) in this codebase is a specialized server-side orchestration layer designed to define and enforce architectural contracts, coding standards, and project-specific constraints for Large Language Models (LLMs). It serves as the authoritative source of truth, ensuring that any system modifications or extensions adhere to the established FSD architecture and semantic guidelines.

philosophy.title: Design Philosophy
philosophy.description: Any modification that violates the established contracts will be rejected by the orchestrator. We prioritize long-term maintainability over short-term speed.

determinism.title: Determinism
determinism.description: Rules must yield identical validation results regardless of the model used.

explicit.title: Explicit Rules
explicit.description: No implicit assumptions; every constraint is defined in the configuration layer.

no_magic.title: No Magic
no_magic.description: Transparent validation logic without hidden heuristics or AI 'guessing'.

validation.title: Validation Over Generation
validation.description: The primary function of the server is to constrain and verify, not to generate code on the fly.

not_items: MCP is NOT a UI or assistant layer
MCP is NOT a tool for autonomous design decisions
MCP is NOT a general-purpose function library
MCP is NOT a replacement for compilation or static typing
MCP is NOT a recommendation engine for 'convenient' development

analysis.description: The LLM queries the MCP server for the current architectural state, including FSD layer boundaries and naming conventions.

contract.description: Before applying changes, the LLM verifies the proposed module structure using project analysis tools.

enforcement.description: MCP restricts the use of forbidden patterns (e.g., direct imports between layers or non-semantic Tailwind classes).

validation.description: The server issues a final 'Success/Failure' status for the updated module, committing changes according to project standards.

capabilities.vs_prompts_description: Unlike static prompts or workflow rules that can be ignored or misinterpreted by models over time, MCP provides a hardcoded validation bridge. It moves knowledge from ephemeral prompt context into a deterministic, executable specification that the model is required to interact with via structured API calls.
